panels.title: Iteration count per asset (top 50)
panels.description: Number of (re)publishes per asset over the time range. The retrospective answer to 'which asset took the most iteration?'

targets.rawSql: SELECT scope_asset AS asset, count(*) AS publishes FROM events WHERE event_type = 'publish.usd' AND status = 'success' AND scope_asset IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 50

panels.title: Crunch fingerprint — publishes per hour, by department
panels.description: Hourly publish count by department. Where the heatmap is hot, that department was crunching at that hour.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('hour', occurred_at) AS time, COALESCE(scope_department, '<no-dept>') AS metric, count(*) AS value FROM events WHERE event_type = 'publish.usd' AND status = 'success' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Frame retry rate (per render job)
panels.description: Failed frames / total frames per render.stats.summary event. Lower is better.

targets.rawSql: SELECT occurred_at AS time, payload->>'renderer' AS metric, CASE WHEN (payload->>'total_frames')::int = 0 THEN 0 ELSE (payload->>'failed_frames')::float / (payload->>'total_frames')::int END AS value FROM events WHERE event_type = 'render.stats.summary' AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Publish duration p50 / p90, by event type
panels.description: Daily p50 and p90 of duration_ms across success events. Spikes suggest tooling or scene-state regressions.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day', occurred_at) AS time, event_type || ' p50' AS metric, percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY duration_ms)::int AS value FROM events WHERE status = 'success' AND duration_ms IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY 1, event_type UNION ALL SELECT date_trunc('day', occurred_at) AS time, event_type || ' p90' AS metric, percentile_cont(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY duration_ms)::int AS value FROM events WHERE status = 'success' AND duration_ms IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(occurred_at) GROUP BY 1, event_type ORDER BY 1
